meta.title: Mastering Redux Toolkit – Slices, Store & Async Thunks (2026)
meta.description: Deep dive into Redux Toolkit. Learn how to use createSlice, configureStore, and createAsyncThunk to build scalable and maintainable React applications.

content.value: Redux Toolkit (RTK) was created to solve three common complaints about Redux: 'Configuring a store is too complicated', 'I have to add a lot of packages to get Redux to do anything useful', and 'Redux requires too much boilerplate code'. RTK is now the official, recommended way to write Redux logic.

A 'Slice' is the collection of Redux reducer logic and actions for a single feature in your app. It uses Immer internally, which allows you to write 'mutative' code that is safely converted into immutable updates.
For API calls, RTK provides `createAsyncThunk`. It generates promise lifecycle action types (pending, fulfilled, and rejected) which you can handle in the `extraReducers` field of your slice.
RTK Query: The Future of Data Fetching

RTK Query is an optional addon included in the Redux Toolkit package. It is a powerful data fetching and caching tool designed to simplify loading data in a web application, eliminating the need to hand-write fetch logic or manage loading states manually.

faq.question: Can I use RTK with existing 'Vanilla' Redux?
faq.answer: Yes. RTK is fully compatible with existing reducers and actions. You can migrate your store to `configureStore` and move logic to slices one piece at a time.

faq.question: Does RTK replace Redux DevTools?
faq.answer: No, it integrates with them perfectly. `configureStore` automatically enables the Redux DevTools extension for you.

faq.question: What is Immer?
faq.answer: Immer is a library that RTK uses to allow you to write code that looks like you're mutating state (e.g., state.push()), but it creates a brand new immutable state object under the hood.

What library does RTK use to allow 'mutative' logic in reducers?
